chapter_context.summary: Pronounces judgment on Pharaoh of Egypt, described as a great dragon (crocodile) lying in his rivers who boasted that the Nile was his, promising to hook him and leave him in the wilderness, followed by Nebuchadnezzar receiving Egypt as wages for Tyre.

outline.text: Judgment on Pharaoh of Egypt: The Great Crocodile in His Rivers
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Judgment on Pharaoh of Egypt: The Great Crocodile in His Rivers (29:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Desolation of Egypt and Her Future Status as the Humblest Kingdom
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Desolation of Egypt and Her Future Status as the Humblest Kingdom (29:8-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Egypt Awarded to Nebuchadnezzar as Wages for Heavy Labor at Tyre
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Egypt Awarded to Nebuchadnezzar as Wages for Heavy Labor at Tyre (29:17-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies Against Pagan Nations
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies against pagan nations, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 29, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
